prompt: A 1925 Bauhaus graphic design poster representing a Support Vector Machine. The composition features a bold, thick black diagonal line cutting across an off-white, textured paper background. On one side of the line, a cluster of vibrant red circles; on the other side, a cluster of deep blue squares. The clusters are separated by a clear, wide 'margin' of empty space along the diagonal line. Minimalist geometric aesthetic, primary colors only (red, blue, yellow, black). High-contrast, flat ink on paper, no gradients, sharp edges, authentic 1920s print quality. No text.
tradition_approach: The composition replicates Bauhaus’s reliance on primary colors, simple geometric forms (circles, squares), and bold black lines. We avoided gradients and centered layouts, prioritizing the movement’s signature asymmetrical, rigid structural grammar.
